title: AmbientBridge
summary: States the formal cone relationship between straight ambient geometry and the associated Poincaré–Einstein metric.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: States the formal cone relationship between straight ambient geometry and the associated Poincaré–Einstein metric.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/AmbientBridge.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The ambient normal coordinate corresponding to a PE radius. In explicit terms, for r in the real numbers, its defining expression is - r to the power 2 divided by 2.
The cone scale coordinate s = r t. In explicit terms, for r, t in the real numbers, its defining expression is r times t.
For r, t in the real numbers, cone Scale r t equals r times t.
correspondence.leanType: theorem coneScale_eq_mul (r t : Real) : coneScale r t = r * t

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Data identifying an FG normal form with a PE normal form. Its mathematical data are: boundary Equiv: X.boundary ≃ M; boundary Metric Adapter: Conf.Metric Field maps to Metric Symbol X.boundary; h R eq: for every r : Real, NF.h R r equals boundary Metric Adapter A.g Rho ambient Rho Of Radial r; ambient T: Function Symbol X.bulk; ambient Rho: Function Symbol X.bulk; s: Function Symbol X.bulk; rho coordinate eq: for every z : X.bulk, ambient Rho z equals ambient Rho Of Radial NF.r.r z; s coordinate eq: for every z : X.bulk, s z equals cone Scale NF.r.r z ambient T z; ds Squared: Metric Symbol X.bulk; ambient Metric In Cone Coordinates: Metric Symbol X.bulk; cone decomposition: ambient Metric In Cone Coordinates equals Metric Symbol.add Metric Symbol.scale By Scalar -1 ds Squared Metric Symbol.scale By Function the function sending z to s z to the power 2 NF.metric.g Plus.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{boundaryEquiv}&\colon X.\operatorname{boundary} ≃ M\\\operatorname{boundaryMetricAdapter}&\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{MetricField} \longrightarrow \operatorname{MetricSymbol} X.\operatorname{boundary}\\\operatorname{hR\_eq}&\colon \forall r \colon \mathbb{R}, \operatorname{NF}.\operatorname{hR} r = \operatorname{boundaryMetricAdapter} (A.\operatorname{gRho} (\operatorname{ambientRhoOfRadial} r))\\\operatorname{ambientT}&\colon \operatorname{FunctionSymbol} X.\operatorname{bulk}\\\operatorname{ambientRho}&\colon \operatorname{FunctionSymbol} X.\operatorname{bulk}\\s&\colon \operatorname{FunctionSymbol} X.\operatorname{bulk}\\\operatorname{rho\_coordinate\_eq}&\colon \forall z \colon X.\operatorname{bulk}, \operatorname{ambientRho} z = \operatorname{ambientRhoOfRadial} (r_{\mathrm{r}} z)\\\operatorname{s\_coordinate\_eq}&\colon \forall z \colon X.\operatorname{bulk}, s z = \operatorname{coneScale} (r_{\mathrm{r}} z) (\operatorname{ambientT} z)\\\operatorname{dsSquared}&\colon \operatorname{MetricSymbol} X.\operatorname{bulk}\\\operatorname{ambientMetricInConeCoordinates}&\colon \operatorname{MetricSymbol} X.\operatorname{bulk}\\\operatorname{cone\_decomposition}&\colon \operatorname{ambientMetricInConeCoordinates} = \operatorname{MetricSymbol}.\operatorname{add} (\operatorname{MetricSymbol}.\operatorname{scaleByScalar} (-1) \operatorname{dsSquared}) (\operatorname{MetricSymbol}.\operatorname{scaleByFunction} (z \longmapsto (s z) ^ 2) \operatorname{NF}.\operatorname{metric}.\operatorname{gPlus})\end{aligned}
correspondence.leanType: structure AmbientPEBridge {M : Type u} {Conf : ConformalStructure.{u, v} M} (A : FG.NormalAmbientData Conf) {X : PESpace.{w, x}} (NF : PoincareNormalFormData.{w, x, y} X)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The slice relation h_r = g_(-r^2/2). In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem boundary_family_from_ambient (B : AmbientPEBridge A NF) (r : Real) : NF.hR r = B.boundaryMetricAdapter (A.gRho (ambientRhoOfRadial r))

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The ambient metric cone decomposition -ds^2 + s^2 gPlus. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambient_metric_cone_decomposition (B : AmbientPEBridge A NF) : B.ambientMetricInConeCoordinates = MetricSymbol.add (MetricSymbol.scaleByScalar (-1) B.dsSquared) (MetricSymbol.scaleByFunction (fun z => (B.s z) ^ 2) NF.metric.gPlus)

references.note: Source for Poincaré normal form and its relation to straight ambient metrics.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
